qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe unserialize in CardDAV group save enables PHP Object Injection and arbitrary file write
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes SQL Injection in Nextmatch filter processing via PHP type juggling bypassing is_int() in Db.php column_data_implode and Base.php parse_search (CWE-89). This finding describes unsafe unserialize() in the CardDAV group save handler at addressbook_groupdav.inc.php:939 (CWE-502). Different vulnerability class, different root cause, different code locations, different exploit path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes SQL Injection in EGroupware's Nextmatch filter processing via PHP type juggling bypassing is_int() checks in Db.php column_data_implode and Base.php parse_search (CWE-89). The sole qualifying finding (carddav_unserialize_rce) describes unsafe unserialize() in the CardDAV group handler (CWE-502), which is a different vulnerability class, different root cause, and different code location. No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.
evidence: Advisory root cause: SQL Injection via PHP type juggling where json_decode converts numeric string keys to integers, bypassing is_int() in Db.php:column_data_implode (~line 1776) and Base.php:parse_search (~line 1134).

carddav_unserialize_rce root cause: bare unserialize() without class restrictions at addressbook_groupdav.inc.php:939 in the CardDAV handler - different code, different vulnerability class (CWE-502 vs CWE-89).
